_comment: Binds the committed SP1 guest ELF to the verifying keys it must be deployed with. verify-vkey-pin.sh asserts sha256(program/elf/teth-pool-prover) == elf_sha256 (always) and, where the SP1 toolchain is present, that the vkey derived from the ELF == program_vkey. A guest rebuild that changes the ELF MUST regenerate the vkey and update this file IN THE SAME COMMIT, then redeploy SP1PoolRootVerifier with the new PROGRAM_VKEY — otherwise every proveStateTransition reverts and all withdrawals brick.
guest_state: MAINNET-READY + throughput optimization + F-2 mint-only pool-tree reserve. F-2: rotate/import (0x62/0x64) refuse the top POOL_TREE_RESERVE=1024 slots so they can't exhaust the headroom the deposit gate keeps for in-flight mints; mint (0x60) keeps full can_insert(); burn/export insert no leaf. groth16_vk_hash unchanged — only the guest state machine changed, not the withdraw/burn circuit. Block input is slim: the guest reads the FULL txid set (verifies the merkle root against the header to authenticate it) then processes only host-provided coinbase + bridge txs, each pinned to its position via compute_txid == txids[idx]; non-bridge txs are never parsed in-circuit (mainnet ~4000-tx blocks collapse to merkle + the bridge ops). Earlier invariants retained: denom canonical/pairwise-distinct + CXFER checked_add; incremental-state public-values tail; 0x2A T_WITHDRAW + 0x60-0x64 bridge ops + 0x22/0x23 CXFER dispatched from the Taproot reveal witness (+ CXFER also from OP_RETURN); BIP141 64-byte-tx reject + coinbase invariant. Rebuilt on the vast prover host (non-Docker); canonical-elf.yml CI rebuilds in the official SP1 Docker image and asserts byte-equality — if CI fails the pin must be regenerated from the Docker artifact before deploy. groth16_vk_hash unchanged (the withdraw/burn circuit did not change).
